 {"id":"bd-37v0","title":"E2E: Integration Test Script for All Audit Fixes","description":"Create a comprehensive end-to-end test script that validates all 4 workstreams and verifies RCH performance requirements from AGENTS.md.

Script location: scripts/audit_e2e.sh (consistent with existing scripts/e2e_test.sh)
Alternatively: tests/audit_integration.rs (Rust integration test)

TEST PLAN:

1. BUILD VERIFICATION:
   - cargo check --all-targets (no compile errors)
   - cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (no warnings)
   - cargo fmt --check (formatting correct)
   - cargo test --all (all unit tests pass)
   - Log: "BUILD: OK" or "BUILD: FAILED at step X"

2. PERFORMANCE BUDGET VERIFICATION (from AGENTS.md lines 250-258):
   - Hook decision for NON-compilation commands: must be <1ms, panic at 5ms
   - Hook decision for compilation commands: must be <5ms, panic at 10ms
   
   Test method:
   - Run classify_command() 100 times on known non-compilation commands (ls, cat, echo, git status)
   - Measure p50, p95, p99 latency
   - FAIL if p99 > 1ms for non-compilation
   
   - Run classify_command() 100 times on known compilation commands (cargo build, make, gcc)
   - Measure p50, p95, p99 latency  
   - FAIL if p99 > 5ms for compilation
   
   Log: "PERF: non-compilation p99={X}ms (<1ms required) {PASS|FAIL}"
   Log: "PERF: compilation p99={X}ms (<5ms required) {PASS|FAIL}"

3. WS1 VERIFICATION (Blocking I/O):
   - Run the hook with a timing history file present
   - Verify spawn_blocking is used (check tokio task metrics if available)
   - Log: timing of load/save operations

4. WS2 VERIFICATION (Allocations):
   - Run classify_command() on 1000 non-compilation commands
   - Measure elapsed time, verify <5ms average
   - Verify reason fields are Cow::Borrowed on reject path (if possible to introspect)
   - Log: "CLASSIFICATION: {N} commands in {T}ms, avg {T/N}ms"

5. WS3 VERIFICATION (Atomics):
   - Create a WorkerState, read all atomic fields concurrently from 10 threads
   - Verify no panics, all values are valid
   - Log: "ATOMIC ACCESS: {N} concurrent reads, 0 errors"

6. WS4 VERIFICATION (Multi-command):
   - Test split_shell_commands with edge cases (quoting, empty, long input)
   - Feed 20 representative multi-command strings through classify_command()
   - Verify expected classification for each
   - Test fail-open: feed malformed input, verify no panic
   - Log each: "MULTI-CMD: '{cmd}' → compilation={bool} (expected={bool}) {PASS|FAIL}"

7. FAIL-OPEN VERIFICATION:
   - Feed intentionally malformed input to the classifier
   - Verify it returns a valid result (not panic/error)
   - Log: "FAIL-OPEN: malformed input handled gracefully {PASS|FAIL}"

8. REGRESSION CHECK:
   - Run full cargo test suite
   - Compare test count before/after (should only increase)
   - Log: "REGRESSION: {passed}/{total} tests passed, {new} new tests added"

OUTPUT FORMAT:
  Each section logs [PASS] or [FAIL] with details.
  Include timestamps for each section.
  Final summary: "AUDIT E2E: X/8 sections passed"
  Exit code 0 if all pass, 1 otherwise.

LOGGING:
  Use tracing or simple println with timestamps.
  All output should be parseable for CI integration.
  Follow existing scripts/e2e_test.sh patterns where applicable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T22:53:49.729766011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T04:13:43.662735016Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T04:13:43.662577473Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-37v0","depends_on_id":"bd-1gyl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-37v0","depends_on_id":"bd-1q0e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-37v0","depends_on_id":"bd-2mmt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-37v0","depends_on_id":"bd-3mog","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-37v0","depends_on_id":"bd-3s1j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-38kz","title":"Phase 1: Foundation - Infrastructure & Context Detection","description":"# Phase 1: Foundation - Infrastructure & Context Detection

## Overview

This phase establishes the foundational infrastructure for rich_rust integration without changing any existing behavior. By the end of this phase, the codebase will have all necessary modules, types, and utilities in place, but rich output will be opt-in via feature flag (defaulting to OFF during development).

## Why This Phase is Critical

The foundation must be rock-solid because:
1. Every subsequent phase builds on these primitives
2. Context detection determines when rich output is safe to use
3. The theme provides visual consistency across all components
4. The feature flag allows emergency rollback

## Key Deliverables

### 1. Dependency Addition
- Add rich_rust to workspace Cargo.toml
- Add to individual crate Cargo.tomls with appropriate features
- Use path dependency during development, crates.io when published

### 2. UI Module Structure
Create the following directory structure:
```
rch-common/src/ui/
  mod.rs       - Re-exports and public API
  context.rs   - OutputContext enum and detection
  theme.rs     - RchTheme with color constants
  icons.rs     - Unicode icons with ASCII fallbacks

rch/src/ui/
  mod.rs       - Re-exports
  console.rs   - RchConsole wrapper

rchd/src/ui/
  mod.rs       - Re-exports
```

### 3. OutputContext Detection
Implement automatic detection of execution context:
- Hook mode: JSON on stdin, should use NO rich output
- Interactive: TTY on stderr, full rich experience
- Colored: No TTY but FORCE_COLOR set, colors only
- Plain: No TTY, no FORCE_COLOR, plain text
- Machine: --json flag or RCH_JSON env var

### 4. RchConsole Wrapper
A context-aware Console that:
- Respects OutputContext settings
- Provides fallback methods (print_or_plain)
- Exposes is_rich() and is_machine() checks

### 5. RchTheme Constants
Define brand colors and semantic styles:
- Primary/Secondary/Accent colors
- Success/Warning/Error/Info semantic colors
- Worker status colors (Healthy/Degraded/Unreachable/etc)
- Text colors (Muted, Dim)

### 6. Feature Flag
- Add 'rich-ui' feature to Cargo.toml
- Default to ON in final release, OFF during development
- Allow compile-time removal of entire rich_rust dependency

## Technical Considerations

### Performance
- Use once_cell::Lazy for static console instances
- Do NOT import UI modules in hook classification path
- Context detection must be <0.1ms

### Compatibility
- Must work on Linux, macOS, Windows
- Must handle terminals without Unicode support
- Must respect NO_COLOR standard (https://no-color.org/)

## Required Unit Tests

### Test File: `rch-common/src/ui/context_tests.rs`

```rust
#[test]
fn test_output_context_from_env_detects_hook_mode() {
    // When RCH_HOOK_MODE=1, should return Hook context
    temp_env::with_var("RCH_HOOK_MODE", Some("1"), || {
        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert_eq!(ctx, OutputContext::Hook);
    });
}

#[test]
fn test_output_context_respects_no_color() {
    // NO_COLOR environment variable must disable colors
    temp_env::with_var("NO_COLOR", Some("1"), || {
        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert!(!ctx.is_colored());
    });
}

#[test]
fn test_output_context_respects_force_color() {
    // FORCE_COLOR should enable colors even without TTY
    temp_env::with_vars([("FORCE_COLOR", Some("1")), ("NO_COLOR", None)], || {
        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert!(ctx.is_colored());
    });
}

#[test]
fn test_output_context_machine_from_json_flag() {
    // --json flag or RCH_JSON=1 should return Machine context
    temp_env::with_var("RCH_JSON", Some("1"), || {
        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert_eq!(ctx, OutputContext::Machine);
    });
}
```

### Test File: `rch-common/src/ui/theme_tests.rs`

```rust
#[test]
fn test_rch_theme_colors_are_valid() {
    let theme = RchTheme::default();
    // All colors should be valid Color enums
    assert!(theme.primary().is_some());
    assert!(theme.success().is_some());
    assert!(theme.error().is_some());
}

#[test]
fn test_worker_status_colors_are_distinct() {
    let theme = RchTheme::default();
    // Status colors should be visually distinct
    assert_ne!(theme.worker_healthy(), theme.worker_degraded());
    assert_ne!(theme.worker_degraded(), theme.worker_unreachable());
}
```

### Test File: `rch-common/src/ui/icons_tests.rs`

```rust
#[test]
fn test_icons_have_ascii_fallbacks() {
    let icons = Icons::new(false); // ASCII mode
    // All icons should have ASCII fallbacks
    assert!(!icons.success().is_empty());
    assert!(!icons.error().is_empty());
    assert!(!icons.warning().is_empty());
}

#[test]
fn test_icons_unicode_vs_ascii_different() {
    let unicode = Icons::new(true);
    let ascii = Icons::new(false);
    // Unicode icons should differ from ASCII
    assert_ne!(unicode.success(), ascii.success());
}
```

### Minimum Test Count: 12 tests
- OutputContext detection: 4 tests
- RchTheme: 3 tests
- Icons: 2 tests
- Integration: 3 tests

## E2E Test Script

### `scripts/e2e_ui_foundation.sh`

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# E2E Test: UI Foundation Infrastructure
# Tests that the UI module correctly detects context and respects env vars

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
LOG_FILE="/tmp/rch_e2e_ui_foundation_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }
log_pass() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] PASS $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }
log_fail() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] FAIL $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; exit 1; }

log "Starting UI Foundation E2E tests"
log "Log file: $LOG_FILE"

# Build
log "Building rch..."
cargo build -p rch --release 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
RCH="$PROJECT_ROOT/target/release/rch"

# Test 1: NO_COLOR disables ANSI
log "Test 1: NO_COLOR environment variable"
OUTPUT=$(NO_COLOR=1 $RCH status 2>&1 || true)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -qP '\x1b\['; then
    log_fail "ANSI codes present despite NO_COLOR=1"
fi
log_pass "NO_COLOR correctly disables ANSI codes"

# Test 2: Hook mode produces no rich output
log "Test 2: Hook mode JSON purity"
HOOK_INPUT='{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo test"}}'
HOOK_OUTPUT=$(echo "$HOOK_INPUT" | RCH_HOOK_MODE=1 $RCH hook --stdin 2>/dev/null)
if ! echo "$HOOK_OUTPUT" | jq -e . >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_fail "Hook output is not valid JSON"
fi
if echo "$HOOK_OUTPUT" | grep -qP '\x1b\['; then
    log_fail "ANSI codes in hook JSON output"
fi
log_pass "Hook mode produces clean JSON"

# Test 3: --json flag produces machine output
log "Test 3: --json flag machine output"
JSON_OUTPUT=$($RCH status --json 2>/dev/null || true)
if echo "$JSON_OUTPUT" | grep -qP '\x1b\['; then
    log_fail "ANSI codes in --json output"
fi
if ! echo "$JSON_OUTPUT" | jq -e . >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_fail "--json output is not valid JSON"
fi
log_pass "--json produces clean machine output"

# Test 4: Feature flag compilation
log "Test 4: rich-ui feature flag"
cargo build -p rch --release --no-default-features 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
log_pass "Builds without rich-ui feature"

log "========================================"
log "All UI Foundation E2E tests passed!"
log "Full log: $LOG_FILE"
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] rich_rust dependency added to workspace
- [ ] UI module structure created in rch-common, rch, rchd
- [ ] OutputContext enum with 5 variants implemented
- [ ] OutputContext::detect() works correctly for all environments
- [ ] RchTheme with all color constants defined
- [ ] Icons with Unicode and ASCII fallbacks
- [ ] 'rich-ui' feature flag compiles without errors
- [ ] NO_COLOR and FORCE_COLOR environment variables respected
- [ ] Unit tests pass: 12+ tests
- [ ] E2E test script passes: `scripts/e2e_ui_foundation.sh`
- [ ] Zero performance impact on hook classification path","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:00:32.925019783Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:30:15.299666060Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:30:15.299518435Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38kz","depends_on_id":"bd-3dv2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:08:28Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-38td","title":"Add TUI keyboard shortcuts for worker actions","description":"# Add Worker Action Keyboard Shortcuts

## Problem
Users must leave TUI to drain/enable workers or cancel builds. The TUI is read-only when it could support common actions.

## Proposed Shortcuts
When Workers panel is selected:
```
d     Drain selected worker (with confirmation)
e     Enable selected worker  
D     Drain ALL workers (with confirmation)
E     Enable ALL workers
r     Refresh worker status immediately
p     Probe selected worker
```

When Active Builds panel is selected:
```
k     Cancel selected build (SIGTERM, with confirmation)
K     Force kill selected build (SIGKILL)
c     Copy build command to clipboard
```

When Build History panel is selected:
```
c     Copy build details to clipboard
Enter View build logs (already works)
/     Filter builds (already works, but undocumented)
```

## Implementation Notes
1. Drain/enable should call daemon API via Unix socket
2. Cancel should send signal to remote process
3. Confirmations should use a modal dialog
4. Actions should show success/error feedback

## API Calls Needed
- POST /workers/{id}/drain
- POST /workers/{id}/enable
- POST /builds/{id}/cancel
- POST /builds/{id}/kill

These APIs should already exist in rchd; we just need to call them from TUI.

## Confirmation Dialog
For destructive actions, show centered modal:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────┐
│ Drain worker 'css'?             │
│                                 │
│ This will stop routing new      │
│ jobs to this worker.            │
│                                 │
│        [Y]es    [N]o            │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/tui/app.rs (event handling, API calls)
- rch/src/tui/state.rs (action state tracking)
- rch/src/tui/widgets.rs (confirmation dialog rendering, help text)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:03:59.226925880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:02:48.705933353Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:02:48.705869114Z","close_reason":"Added worker/build action shortcuts (D/E drain/enable all, x/X cancel/force-kill build) with confirmation dialog modal. Added cancel_build/force_kill_build daemon APIs. 10 new event tests, 7 new state tests, 4 new widget tests. All 1582 tests pass, clippy clean. Used x/X instead of k/K for cancel to avoid vim navigation conflict.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38td","depends_on_id":"bd-1olr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-38td","depends_on_id":"bd-ryot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":85,"issue_id":"bd-38td","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

3. `test_k_cancels_selected_build()`
4. `test_K_force_kills_build()`
5. `test_c_copies_to_clipboard()`
6. `test_confirmation_modal_renders()`
7. `test_confirmation_y_confirms()`

### E2E Test Script (scripts/test_tui_actions.sh)
```bash

# Test 3: Confirmation modal
log "Test 3: Verify confirmation appears for destructive actions"
rch dashboard --test-mode --inject-key d --dump-state 2>&1 | grep -q "confirm" || {
  log "FAIL: No confirmation for drain"
  exit 1
}

log "TUI action tests complete"
```

- Log action request at INFO level
- Log confirmation response at DEBUG level
- Log API call result at DEBUG level
- Log errors at ERROR level with context","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:57:19Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-39fv","title":"Update dependencies to latest stable versions","description":"# Update Dependencies to Latest Stable Versions

## Purpose
Systematically update all workspace dependencies to their latest stable versions using the library-updater skill.

## Scope
All Cargo.toml files across the workspace:
- `/Cargo.toml` (workspace root)
- `/rch/Cargo.toml` (hook CLI)
- `/rchd/Cargo.toml` (daemon)
- `/rch-wkr/Cargo.toml` (worker agent)
- `/rch-common/Cargo.toml` (shared library)
- `/rch-telemetry/Cargo.toml` (telemetry)

## Process
1. Run `cargo update` to update Cargo.lock
2. Use library-updater skill to check for major version updates
3. Update explicit version pins where safe
4. Run full test suite to verify compatibility
5. Check for deprecation warnings and update usage patterns

## Verification
```bash
cargo check --all-targets
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test
```

## Notes
- Merged from bd-3f9a (duplicate task)
- Priority: P1 - important for security patches and performance improvements

 {"id":"bd-3dv2","title":"Epic: rich_rust Integration - Premium Terminal Output for RCH","description":"# Epic: rich_rust Integration - Premium Terminal Output for RCH

## Executive Summary

This epic encompasses the complete integration of the rich_rust library into RCH (Remote Compilation Helper) to provide beautiful, premium terminal output for human operators watching AI coding agents work. The integration must maintain **zero interference** with AI agents, which are the primary users of RCH.

## Background & Motivation

### The Problem
RCH currently outputs plain text for status, errors, and progress. While functional, this provides a poor experience for humans monitoring AI agents. When operators run 15+ agents simultaneously, they need at-a-glance visual feedback about:
- Which workers are healthy/degraded/offline
- What compilations are in progress
- Where errors occurred and how to fix them
- Transfer and benchmark progress

### The Solution
Integrate rich_rust (a Rust port of Python's Rich library) to provide:
- Styled text with markup syntax ([bold red]text[/])
- Tables with auto-sizing columns and Unicode borders
- Panels for framed content (errors, status)
- Progress bars for transfers and benchmarks
- Color-coded status indicators
- Automatic terminal capability detection and color downgrade

## The Cardinal Rule: Agent-First Architecture

**CRITICAL**: AI coding agents are the PRIMARY users of RCH. They interact through:
1. JSON hook protocol (stdin -> stdout)
2. Exit codes (0, 1, 101, 128+N)
3. Streamed compilation output

Rich formatting must NEVER interfere with these machine interfaces. The design principle is:
- **stderr for humans**: Rich output goes to stderr
- **stdout for machines**: Structured data stays pristine on stdout
- **Graceful degradation**: If rich_rust fails, fall back to plain text

## Output Classification

### NEVER Add Rich Formatting (Machine Output)
- Hook JSON responses (stdout)
- --json flag output (stdout)
- Compilation output passthrough (stdout)
- Test output streaming (stdout)
- Exit codes (process exit status)

### ADD Rich Formatting (Human Output)
- rch status commands (stderr)
- rch workers list/probe/benchmark (stderr)
- rchd startup banner and health (stderr)
- Error messages and warnings (stderr)
- Transfer and benchmark progress (stderr)

## Implementation Phases

1. **Phase 1: Foundation** - Infrastructure, modules, context detection
2. **Phase 2: CLI Commands** - Rich output for status/worker commands
3. **Phase 3: Daemon UI** - Startup banner, health displays
4. **Phase 4: Error Experience** - Beautiful error panels
5. **Phase 5: Progress & Feedback** - Progress bars, spinners
6. **Phase 6: Polish** - Theme refinement, docs, validation

## Success Criteria

1. All rch CLI commands have beautiful, informative output
2. AI agents function identically with rich output enabled/disabled
3. NO_COLOR and FORCE_COLOR environment variables are respected
4. Performance: Zero overhead on hook classification hot path
5. All existing tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:00:06.187551979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T07:08:31.061597056Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T07:08:31.061493863Z","close_reason":"EPIC COMPLETE: rich_rust Integration - Premium Terminal Output for RCH. All 6 phases closed: Phase 1 (Foundation), Phase 2 (CLI Commands), Phase 3 (Daemon UI), Phase 4 (Error Experience), Phase 5 (Progress & Feedback), Phase 6 (Polish & Documentation). RCH now has beautiful, context-aware terminal output that works correctly with both humans and AI agents.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3eaa","title":"Worker Preflight Health Guards (Load + Disk)","description":"# Worker Preflight Health Guards (Load Average + Disk Space)

## Merged Scope
This bead consolidates two related worker preflight check features:
- **Load Average Guard** (formerly bd-2hbf): Skip workers with high CPU load
- **Disk Space Guard** (formerly bd-1mij): Skip workers with low free disk

Both are preflight checks that improve worker selection quality by filtering out unhealthy workers before job assignment.

## Background
Even if slots are available, a worker may be unsuitable:
- High load average spikes hurt build times
- Low disk space can cause builds to fail or slow dramatically

Preflight guards prevent wasted transfers and improve overall compilation success rates.

## Goals
1. Skip workers whose load average exceeds a threshold (per-core normalized)
2. Skip workers whose free disk space is below minimum
3. Provide clear selection reasons (NoWorkersWithCapacity, NoWorkersWithDisk)
4. Fail-open: if all workers exceed thresholds, pick best available with warning

## Design / Approach

### Configuration
```toml
[selection]
max_load_per_core = 2.0    # Skip if load1 / cores > threshold
min_free_gb = 10.0         # Skip if free disk < threshold
```

### Telemetry Fields
Extend worker capabilities/telemetry:
- `load_avg_1`, `load_avg_5`, `load_avg_15` (from /proc/loadavg or uptime)
- `disk_free_gb`, `disk_total_gb` (from df or statfs)

### Selection Logic
1. Filter by slot availability
2. Filter by load-per-core threshold
3. Filter by disk space threshold
4. Sort by SpeedScore
5. If all filtered out, pick best available with warning

## Tasks / Subtasks
1. Extend telemetry payload to include load avg and disk metrics
2. Add fallback SSH probe (uptime, df) when telemetry unavailable
3. Add config fields with validation
4. Update selection logic with reason codes
5. Expose in `rch status` and `rch workers list` output
6. Add warnings when using degraded workers

## Tests

### Unit Tests
- `rchd/src/selection.rs`: load-per-core threshold logic, disk space filter
- `rchd/src/telemetry.rs`: load avg parsing, disk metric extraction
- Selection reason codes for each guard type

### Integration Tests
- Mock worker reports high load -> skipped
- Mock worker reports low disk -> skipped
- All workers degraded -> pick best with warning

### E2E Tests
Script: `scripts/e2e_preflight_guards.sh`

Scenarios:
- Mixed load -> skip high, select low
- Mixed disk -> skip low-disk, select adequate
- All high load -> select least-loaded with warning
- All low disk -> select most-available with warning
- Status output displays load and disk warnings

## Acceptance Criteria
- High-load workers are skipped automatically
- Low-disk workers are skipped automatically
- Selection reasons are visible and actionable
- Thresholds are configurable and documented
- Fail-open behavior preserves system usability

## Logging & E2E
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, duration_ms, result, error
- Phases: classify, select (with guard reasons), sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary

## Edge Cases & UX
- Missing telemetry -> allow worker but warn
- Use load-per-core to normalize across heterogeneous machines
- Support per-worker threshold overrides
- Selection reason includes specific guard signals

## Dependencies
- Depends on bd-155i (Worker capabilities report + mismatch warnings)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"WhiteLake","created_at":"2026-01-26T00:30:20.175037620Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:55:28.801894925Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T05:55:28.801827188Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3eaa","depends_on_id":"bd-155i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:08:28Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-3eaa","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Progress - Phase 1 Complete

### Changes Made

1. **WorkerCapabilities extended** (rch-common/src/types.rs)
   - Added health metrics: `num_cpus`, `load_avg_1`, `load_avg_5`, `load_avg_15`
   - Added disk metrics: `disk_free_gb`, `disk_total_gb`
   - Added helper methods: `load_per_core()`, `is_high_load()`, `is_low_disk()`

2. **SelectionConfig extended** (rch-common/src/types.rs)
   - Added `max_load_per_core: Option<f64>` (default: 2.0)
   - Added `min_free_gb: Option<f64>` (default: 10.0)

3. **Worker probing** (rch-wkr/src/main.rs)
   - Added `probe_num_cpus()` - nproc (Linux) or sysctl (macOS)
   - Added `probe_load_average()` - /proc/loadavg or uptime
   - Added `probe_disk_space()` - df -P -k /tmp

4. **Selection logic** (rchd/src/selection.rs)
   - Added preflight filtering in `get_eligible_workers()`
   - Workers exceeding thresholds are skipped with debug logging
   - Workers kept for fail-open fallback

5. **Unit tests** (rch-common/src/types.rs)
   - test_load_per_core_calculation
   - test_is_high_load
   - test_is_low_disk
   - test_selection_config_preflight_defaults
   - test_selection_config_preflight_serde
   - test_worker_capabilities_health_metrics_serde

### All Tests Passing
- rch-common: 723 tests
- rchd: 23 tests
- All crates compile

### Remaining
- CLI output: Show load/disk info in `rch workers list` (optional)
- E2E test script","created_at":"2026-01-27T05:55:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3emi","title":"Unit Tests: Common Library (No Mocks)","description":"Add comprehensive unit tests for rch-common library without mock infrastructure.

- rch-common/src/toolchain.rs - Toolchain detection

- rch-common/tests/ - Has integration tests but gaps remain

## Test Requirements
1. **Remote Compilation Tests**
   - Test command translation
   - Test argument handling
   - Test path mapping
   - Test error wrapping

2. **Toolchain Detection Tests**
   - Test rustc version parsing
   - Test cargo detection
   - Test gcc/clang detection
   - Test bun/node detection
   - Test missing toolchain handling

## Constraints
- NO MockFileSystem for path operations
- Use real file system with temp dirs
- Test actual toolchain detection
- Skip tests if toolchain not installed (graceful)

## Logging Requirements
- Each test: TEST START/TEST PASS format
- Log detected toolchain versions
- Include PATH and env on detection failures","notes":"Verified 680 tests (675 unit + 5 integration) in rch-common. -{"id":"bd-3ewx","title":"Split commands.rs into modular command files","description":"# Split commands.rs into Modules

## Problem
rch/src/commands.rs is 10,432 lines - too large to navigate, review, or maintain effectively.

## Current Structure
```
commands.rs
├── Response types (lines 93-582, ~490 lines)
├── Helper functions (lines 150-250, 396-416, ~120 lines)
└── Command implementations (lines 600-10432, ~9800 lines)
    ├── Config commands (~800 lines)
    ├── Daemon commands (~600 lines)
    ├── Workers commands (~2000 lines)
    ├── Fleet commands (~1000 lines)
    ├── Hook commands (~500 lines)
    ├── Queue/Cancel commands (~400 lines)
    ├── Self-test commands (~800 lines)
    ├── Update commands (~500 lines)
    ├── Agents commands (~300 lines)
    └── Misc helpers (~2900 lines)
```

## Proposed Structure
```
rch/src/commands/
├── mod.rs              # Re-exports, shared imports
├── types.rs            # Response structs (WorkerInfo, etc.)
├── helpers.rs          # Shared utilities
├── daemon.rs           # start, stop, restart, status, logs
├── workers.rs          # list, probe, drain, enable, benchmark, etc.
├── config.rs           # show, init, validate, set, lint, etc.
├── fleet.rs            # deploy, drain, status, upgrade
├── hook.rs             # install, uninstall, test
├── queue.rs            # queue, cancel
├── self_test.rs        # self-test command
├── update.rs           # update command
└── agents.rs           # agents commands
```

## Migration Strategy

### Phase 1: Extract Types (no behavior change)
1. Create commands/types.rs
2. Move all response structs
3. Update imports in commands.rs
4. Verify tests pass

### Phase 2: Extract Helpers
1. Create commands/helpers.rs
2. Move shared functions
3. Make public what's needed
4. Verify tests pass

### Phase 3: Extract Command Groups (one at a time)
1. Create commands/daemon.rs
2. Move daemon command handlers
3. Re-export from mod.rs
4. Verify tests pass
5. Repeat for each command group

## Public API Preservation
The extraction must not change the public API:
- Same function signatures
- Same error types
- Same command behavior
- Same test coverage

## Files to Create
- rch/src/commands/mod.rs
- rch/src/commands/types.rs
- rch/src/commands/helpers.rs
- rch/src/commands/daemon.rs
- rch/src/commands/workers.rs
- rch/src/commands/config.rs
- rch/src/commands/fleet.rs
- rch/src/commands/hook.rs
- rch/src/commands/queue.rs
- rch/src/commands/self_test.rs
- rch/src/commands/update.rs
- rch/src/commands/agents.rs

## Files to Delete (after migration)
- rch/src/commands.rs (replaced by commands/mod.rs)

## Testing
- All existing tests must pass
- No new warnings from clippy
- Build time should not increase significantly","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:11:11.313521582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T01:27:51.682178886Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ewx","depends_on_id":"bd-lym6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:45:34Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":99,"issue_id":"bd-3ewx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
Each extracted module needs its own test file:
1. `rch/src/commands/types_test.rs` - All type serde roundtrips
2. `rch/src/commands/helpers_test.rs` - All helper functions
3. `rch/src/commands/daemon_test.rs` - Daemon command handlers
4. `rch/src/commands/workers_test.rs` - Worker command handlers
5. `rch/src/commands/config_test.rs` - Config command handlers

### Integration Tests
1. All existing tests must continue passing
2. No new clippy warnings
3. Module imports work correctly

### Regression Test Script (scripts/test_commands_refactor.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG_FILE=${RCH_TEST_LOG:-/tmp/rch_refactor_test.log}

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

log "Testing commands.rs refactor"

# Test 1: All tests pass
log "Test 1: cargo test"
cargo test -p rch 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

# Test 2: No clippy warnings
log "Test 2: cargo clippy"
cargo clippy -p rch --all-targets -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

# Test 3: No unused imports/dead code
log "Test 3: Check for dead code"
cargo check -p rch 2>&1 | grep -i "unused\|dead_code" && {
  log "WARN: Unused code detected"
}

# Test 4: API unchanged
log "Test 4: Verify public API unchanged"
cargo doc -p rch --no-deps 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

# Test 5: Module file sizes
log "Test 5: Verify no file exceeds 2000 lines"
for f in rch/src/commands/*.rs; do
  lines=$(wc -l < "$f")
  if [ "$lines" -gt 2000 ]; then
    log "FAIL: $f has $lines lines (max 2000)"
    exit 1
  fi
  log "OK: $f has $lines lines"
done

log "Refactor tests complete"
```

### Logging Requirements
- Document module structure in code comments
- Log module load times at TRACE level (for build analysis)","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:58:20Z"},{"id":104,"issue_id":"bd-3ewx","author":"DarkMeadow","text":"Phase 2 helpers extraction progress by DarkMeadow: added default_socket_path to helpers.rs, removed 5 duplicate SSH helpers from mod.rs, updated imports, fixed test module. mod.rs -139 lines. Tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-01-30T22:18:40Z"},{"id":105,"issue_id":"bd-3ewx","author":"DarkMeadow","text":"Phase 3 progress: Extracted daemon commands to daemon.rs (daemon_status, daemon_start, daemon_stop, daemon_restart, daemon_reload, daemon_logs, which_rchd). mod.rs reduced from 11,511 to 10,773 lines (-738 lines). All 63 tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-01-30T22:31:55Z"},{"id":106,"issue_id":"bd-3ewx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Phase 3 daemon extraction complete by SilverMist: Created daemon.rs (754 lines) with all 6 daemon commands + which_rchd helper. Next: Continue extracting remaining command groups.","created_at":"2026-01-30T22:32:10Z"},{"id":107,"issue_id":"bd-3ewx","author":"CalmFinch","text":"CalmFinch progress: Created workers.rs module structure with imports. Fixed config/status imports. mod.rs: 6812→3729 lines. 63 tests pass. Target: under 2000 lines. Next: workers_deploy, workers_setup, workers_init, workers_discover.","created_at":"2026-01-31T01:18:28Z"},{"id":111,"issue_id":"bd-3ewx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Session progress summary: Extracted agents.rs (364 lines, 4 functions) and speedscore.rs (450 lines, speedscore command). mod.rs reduced from 6812→3729 lines. All 63 tests pass, clippy clean. Remaining to reach 2000: ~1729 lines to extract. Candidates: workers_deploy_binary (~470), workers_setup (~546), workers_init (~331), workers_discover (~412). Note: config.rs at 2389 lines also exceeds 2000 limit.","created_at":"2026-01-31T01:20:17Z"},{"id":112,"issue_id":"bd-3ewx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TopazLynx progress: Extracted workers_init.rs (584 lines) with workers_init and workers_discover functions. mod.rs now at 3107 lines (target: <2000). Remaining: ~1107 lines to extract from mod.rs.","created_at":"2026-01-31T01:27:28Z"},{"id":113,"issue_id":"bd-3ewx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Session complete summary: mod.rs reduced from ~6800 to 3107 lines.  {"id":"bd-3fda","title":"Standardize TUI status indicators using StatusIndicator enum","description":"# Standardize TUI Status Indicators

## Problem
The TUI uses multiple icon sources inconsistently:

### In widgets.rs lines 154-160 (header status):
```rust
let status_text = match state.daemon.status {
    Status::Running => "● Running",
    Status::Stopped => "○ Stopped",
    Status::Error => "✗ Error",
    Status::Unknown => "? Unknown",
};
```

### In widgets.rs lines 237-242 (worker status):
```rust
let status_icon = match w.status {
    WorkerStatus::Healthy => "●",
    WorkerStatus::Degraded => "◐",
    WorkerStatus::Unreachable => "○",
    WorkerStatus::Draining => "◑",
};
```

### In CLI commands (using StatusIndicator):
```rust
StatusIndicator::Success.display(style)  // Returns themed ✓
StatusIndicator::Error.display(style)    // Returns themed ✗
```

## Impact
- Inconsistent appearance between TUI and CLI
- TUI doesn't respect theme settings
- Hardcoded symbols don't adapt to terminal capabilities
- Accessibility features in StatusIndicator not used

## Solution
1. Import StatusIndicator into TUI widgets
2. Create TUI-specific display method that returns &str for ratatui
3. Replace all hardcoded symbols with StatusIndicator calls
4. Ensure colors are sourced from ColorScheme

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/tui/widgets.rs
- rch/src/ui/theme.rs (may need TUI-specific display method)

## Testing
- Verify TUI matches CLI appearance
- Test with --high-contrast mode
- Test with color-blind palettes
- Verify symbol consistency","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:03:16.913248958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:33:33.504121012Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:33:33.504057734Z","close_reason":"Standardized TUI status indicators using StatusIndicator enum and Symbols struct. Replaced all hardcoded symbols in widgets.rs (daemon header, worker status, active builds, build history) with centralized Symbols::UNICODE and StatusIndicator-based lookups. Added indicator_color helper for ratatui Color bridging. All symbols now respect theme/accessibility settings. 5 new integration tests. All 1719 tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3fda","depends_on_id":"bd-lcoa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3fda","depends_on_id":"bd-ryot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"}]}
